[Question]
What could be the possible causes of toothache?

There are several possible causes of toothache, including:

1. Tooth decay or cavities: Bacteria in the mouth can cause acid to build up and damage the enamel of the teeth, leading to pain.
2. Gum disease: Inflammation of the gums (gingivitis) or infection of the gum tissue (periodontitis) can cause pain and discomfort.
3. Cracked or fractured teeth: A crack or fracture in a tooth can expose the pulp, causing pain.
4. Abscesses: Pus-filled pockets that form when bacteria infect the pulp of a tooth can cause severe pain.
5. Grinding or clenching teeth: Excessive grinding or clenching of the teeth can lead to jaw pain and toothache.
6. Sinus pressure: Sinus infections or allergies can cause referred pain in the teeth.
7. Dental work: Recent dental procedures, such as fillings or crowns, can cause temporary pain.
8. TMJ disorders: Problems with the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which connects the jawbone to the skull, can cause pain in the face and teeth.
